expressions.notes: 자리 = space, spot, seat, position; occasion

expressions.english: It feels good to sit down, right?
expressions.notes: 편하다 = to be comfortable; to be convenient

expressions.english: There is no place to sit.
My legs hurt. I want to sit down.
You can't sit in the seats reserved for the elderly.
expressions.notes: 노약자석 = seats reserved for the elderly

expressions.english: Shall we sit down for a second and talk?
expressions.notes:
우리 -(으)ㄹ까요? = Shall we ...?
이야기하다 = to talk, speak, say, tell; to have a conversation; to tell a story

expressions.english: Who is that person sitting there?

expressions.korean: 여기 앉아 있던 사람 못 봤어요?
expressions.english: Did you see the person who was sitting here?

expressions.korean: 저 벤치에 앉아서 좀 쉬었다 가요.
expressions.english: Let's sit down on that bench there and rest before we walk again.

expressions.korean: 지기요. 안 보이니까 좀 앉아 주세요.
expressions.english: Excuse me, I can't see (because of you), so please sit down.

expressions.korean: 가만히 앉아 있으면 문제가 해결되지 앉아요.
expressions.english: If you just sit there doing nothing, the problem won't be solved.
expressions.notes:
가만히 = still; motionlessly
문제 = problem; issue; thing; matter
해결되다 = to be solved

expressions.korean: 지금 이 시간에 버스에 타면 앉을 자리 가 있을까요?
expressions.english: If you take the bus at this hour, will there be any seats left?
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
지금 이 시간 = at this hour
타다 = to ride; to get on; to take (vehicle)

expressions.korean: 어제 책상에 앉아서 공부하다가 잠이 들어 버렸어요.
expressions.english: I fell asleep yesterday while sitting in the chair and studying.
expressions.notes:
책상 = desk
공부하다 = to study
잠 = sleep
잠이 들다 = to fall asleep
-아/어/여 버리다 is used when talking about something that happened against your hope or expectations.

expressions.korean: 가만히 앉아서 컴퓨터만 하지 말고 밖에 나가서 운동도 하세요.
expressions.english: Don't just sit there using your computer, but also go out and do some exercise.
expressions.notes:
컴퓨터 = computer
운동 = exercise; sports
-도 = too, also
운동하다 = to exercise, work out
